questions.text: A number that can be expressed in the form p/q, where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0, is called:
questions.explanation: This is the formal definition of a rational number.

questions.text: Which of the following is a negative rational number?
questions.explanation: A rational number is negative if its numerator and denominator have opposite signs.
Divide both by their HCF (5) and move the negative sign to the numerator: -3/5.

questions.text: What is the additive inverse of -2/3?
questions.explanation: The additive inverse of a/b is -a/b, such that their sum is zero.

questions.text: How many rational numbers are there between any two rational numbers?
questions.explanation: There are infinitely many rational numbers between any two given rational numbers.

questions.text: What is the value of 0 divided by any non-zero rational number?
questions.explanation: 0 divided by any non-zero number is always 0.

questions.text: Two rational numbers are equivalent if they have the same:
questions.explanation: Equivalent rational numbers represent the same value when reduced to their simplest (standard) form.
The reciprocal of a number 'n' is 1/n. For -5, it is -1/5.
